title: Planck constant
short: The fundamental constant linking a photon's energy to its frequency, h ≈ 6.63 × 10⁻³⁴ J·s.
full:
Planck's constant h is the proportionality between a photon's energy and its frequency, E = h·f. Its extreme smallness, h ≈ 6.63 × 10⁻³⁴ J·s, is why quantum effects are invisible in everyday life — the energy steps are tiny.

It is one of the cornerstones of quantum mechanics; the calculator substitutes it for you.

The SI unit is the joule-second (J·s).
